findings.acceptance_test: By the end of the first page of chapter 16, a reader can state when the scene occurs relative to the summit and why this confession is the next consequential move.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Chapter 16 owns the miss because later chapters own missing boundary re-orientation, and the dislocation appears the moment this chapter opens.
findings.problem: Chapter 15 ends on the unresolved father-daughter aftermath of Falk's reveal and stacks multiple fresh costs at once, then chapter 16 opens at Damian's apartment with no time marker or priority rationale. Coming straight off snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4265 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4273, the reader has to guess whether hours, days, or longer have passed and why Anya is resolving Damian first instead of the Loid, Sylvia, and Yuri fallout that chapter 15 just made urgent. Chapter 17 later backfills that Sylvia confronted her two weeks earlier at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4593, which confirms the missing orientation rather than supplying it when the handoff is needed.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the first paragraph of chapter 16, anchor elapsed time from the summit and name the pressure ordering. One sentence is enough if it tells the reader whether Anya has already faced Sylvia or Loid, or is postponing them, and why Damian cannot wait.

findings.acceptance_test: Before or during the dinner scene, the reader gets a clear indication of what changed between the summit wall scene and Loid's calmer domestic scrutiny, so the later warmth reads as negotiated rather than assumed.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Chapter 17 owns the gap because it asks the reader to accept the calmer Loid and Anya register without supplying the intervening emotional bridge.
findings.problem: Chapter 15 frames Loid's discovery of Anya's false report as the most intimate private cost in the summit fallout: he recognizes his own methods in her at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4227 and ends in a cold, unfinished wall scene at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4267. Chapter 16 then gives its full dramatic aftermath to Damian instead. By chapter 17, Loid and Anya are back in lightly comic dinner-interrogation mode at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4695, and the Loid beat has shifted to his possible long-term telepathy pattern recognition at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4757. The window never supplies the bridge that turns the father-daughter rupture into this calmer register, so the emotional thread feels swapped rather than carried.
findings.rewrite_direction: Give chapter 16 or early chapter 17 one concrete aftermath beat that shows how Loid and Anya have been living with Falk's reveal. It can stay restrained, but it needs to make the dinner détente feel provisional and earned instead of simply time-skipped.

findings.acceptance_test: After reading chapter 17, the reader can explain why Yuri's silence still matters despite chapter 15 establishing camera evidence that should outrun his lie.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Chapter 17 owns the contradiction because the later chapter reopens Yuri's report choice as suspense after chapter 15 established that camera review should outrun the lie.
findings.problem: Chapter 15 explicitly says Yuri's cover story would not survive review because the terrace camera footage would identify Yor within twenty-four hours. Weeks later, chapter 17 still frames the live uncertainty as whether Yuri will report what he witnessed or omit it from his file. On a consecutive read, those two claims do not line up: either the footage was suppressed, buried, or never reviewed, or Yuri's personal report is no longer the decisive exposure point, but the later chapter does not tell the reader which.
findings.rewrite_direction: Clarify in chapter 17 whether the camera record was scrubbed, buried, or procedurally ignored, or else soften chapter 15's certainty about twenty-four-hour identification. Keep Yuri's choice meaningful by specifying what only his testimony can add beyond the footage.

findings.acceptance_test: In this three-chapter window, the 'specific' or 'particular' plus abstract-noun construction appears no more than once or twice, with the other beats rephrased into concrete image or action.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Chapter 17 owns the cleanup because the later iterations are what make the repetition visible at window scale, and they can be revised without disturbing earlier scene logic.
findings.problem: Across these three consecutive chapters, the prose repeatedly classifies perception with the same analytic template instead of varying the sentence shape: 'a specific weight' at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4079, 'specific to him' at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4286, 'the specific worry of a son' at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4577, 'the specific weight' again at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4729, and 'the particular impression' at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4765. Read back-to-back, the repetition becomes audible as authorial phrasing rather than character-specific perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the later instances first. Strip the 'specific' or 'particular' tag where the image, action, or thought already makes the distinction clear, and vary the syntax so adjacent chapters are not all classifying feeling in the same way.

summary: Dominant failure: chapter 15 loads multi-front fallout, but the window narrows toward Anya and Damian so abruptly that the 15/16 handoff and the Loid/Yuri carryover lose clarity.
